vulnerability_remediation_workflow.overview: This workflow guides you through fixing failing unit tests after a DevOps engineer has created a PR to fix a vulnerability (CVE patch or package upgrade). This mode is designed for automated execution via bob-shell in GitHub Actions workflows.

workflow_context.trigger: GitHub Actions workflow after unit tests fail on a vulnerability remediation PR
workflow_context.input_format: The workflow prompt provides: - PR number in the first line: "Fix the failing unit tests in PR #[number]" - PR URL in step 1: "https://github.com/[org]/[repo]/pull/[number]" - PR branch name in step 6: "Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: [branch-name]"

phase.name: parse_workflow_prompt
phase.title: Parse Workflow Prompt for PR Details
phase.description: Extract PR URL and branch name from the workflow prompt provided by GitHub Actions

step.action: Extract PR URL from workflow prompt
step.example: From: "1. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/highorbit25/concert-bob-remediate/pull/38" Extract: - owner: highorbit25 - repo: concert-bob-remediate - pr_number: 38

step.action: Extract PR branch name from workflow prompt
step.pattern: Step 6 contains: "Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: [branch-name]"

extract.pr_branch: The exact branch name to commit to

step.example: From: "6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask" Extract: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
step.critical: This branch name MUST be used for all git operations. Never create a new branch.

phase.name: pr_analysis
phase.title: Analyze PR and Understand Security Fix
phase.description: Fetch PR details using GitHub MCP and understand what vulnerability is being fixed

step.action: Fetch PR details via GitHub MCP
step.retrieve: - PR title and description - Changed files list - Diff of changes - CI/CD test results - Any linked issues or CVE references

checklist.item: What is the old version and new version?
Is this a direct or transitive dependency?
What is the severity of the vulnerability?

step.details: Review changes in dependency files: - requirements.txt (Python) - package.json (Node.js) - pom.xml (Java/Maven) - build.gradle (Java/Gradle) - Gemfile (Ruby) - go.mod (Go)
Look for: - Release notes of the upgraded package - Migration guides between versions - Deprecated APIs or methods - Changed method signatures - New required parameters - Removed functionality

tools.tool: read_file - Read CHANGELOG or release notes if available
search_files - Search for usage of the upgraded package

phase.name: test_execution
phase.title: Run Tests and Identify Failures
phase.description: Execute the unit test suite to see which tests are failing and why

step.details: Capture the full test output including: - Which tests failed - Error messages and stack traces - Expected vs actual behavior - Any warnings or deprecation notices

analysis_checklist.item: Are failures related to the upgraded package?
What are the common error patterns?

phase.name: root_cause_analysis
phase.title: Determine Root Cause of Test Failures
phase.description: Investigate why tests are failing and identify what needs to be fixed

pattern.name: import_errors
pattern.description: Module or package import paths have changed
pattern.solution_approach: - Check package documentation for new import paths - Search for renamed modules or functions - Update import statements in application code

pattern.name: api_signature_changes
pattern.description: Function or method signatures have changed
pattern.example: TypeError: function() takes 2 positional arguments but 3 were given
pattern.solution_approach: - Review function documentation for new signature - Check for new required parameters - Check for removed or renamed parameters - Update function calls throughout codebase

pattern.name: deprecated_apis
pattern.description: APIs that were used are now deprecated or removed
pattern.solution_approach: - Find replacement APIs in documentation - Update code to use new recommended methods - Ensure new methods provide same functionality

pattern.name: behavior_changes
pattern.description: Package behavior has changed causing different results
pattern.solution_approach: - Review release notes for behavior changes - Determine if test expectations need updating - Check if application logic needs adjustment - Verify security fix is not compromised

step.details: Understand what the test is trying to verify and how it's failing

step.action: Read the application code being tested
step.details: Examine the code that uses the upgraded package

step.action: Search for all usages of the upgraded package
step.details: Find all places in the codebase that import or use the package

phase.name: code_remediation
phase.title: Implement Code Fixes
phase.description: Make targeted changes to fix the failing tests while maintaining the security fix

principles.principle: Security First - Never revert or compromise the security fix
Minimal Changes - Only change what's necessary to fix tests
One Issue at a Time - Fix failures incrementally
Test After Each Fix - Verify each change works before moving on

step.details: Start with: 1. Import errors (blocking other tests) 2. API signature changes (widespread impact) 3. Deprecated API usage (warnings) 4. Behavior changes (may need test updates)

tools.tool: apply_diff - For surgical edits to existing code
write_to_file - For complete file rewrites if needed

best_practices.practice: Make multiple changes in one diff when possible

step.details: After each fix, run the test suite to verify: - The specific test now passes - No new tests are broken - No regressions introduced
Repeat steps 2-3 for each failing test or group of related failures. Keep track of what's been fixed and what remains.
Once all known failures are fixed, run the complete test suite to ensure no regressions and all tests pass.

phase.name: validation
phase.title: Validate and Document Fixes
phase.description: Ensure all fixes are correct, tests pass, and changes are well-documented

step.details: Confirm that: - The upgraded package version is still in place - No workarounds that compromise security were added - The CVE is actually fixed by the changes

documentation_requirements.item: Explain why each change was necessary
Reference the breaking changes that caused failures
Note any potential impacts or considerations

phase.name: pr_update
phase.title: Update PR with Fixes
phase.description: Commit changes to the PR branch and document the fixes

critical_requirement.rule: ALWAYS commit to the branch name provided in step 6 of the workflow prompt
critical_requirement.why: - The workflow explicitly provides the correct branch name - This ensures the PR is updated (not a new PR created) - This triggers the CI/CD workflow to re-run tests

step.action: Review all changes one final time
step.details: Use obtain_git_diff to see all local changes before committing

step.critical: Use the exact branch name from step 6 of the workflow prompt. This updates the existing PR and triggers the CI/CD workflow.

step.action: Add comment to PR documenting fixes

use_mcp_tool.arguments: { "method": "create", "owner": "highorbit25", "repo": "concert-bob-remediate", "pullNumber": 38, "event": "COMMENT", "body": "## Unit Test Fixes\n\nI've fixed the failing unit tests..." }

success_criteria.criterion: PR is updated with fixes and explanation

scenario.name: cannot_fix_without_breaking_security
scenario.description: Tests cannot be fixed without reverting the security patch
scenario.action: - Document the conflict clearly - Explain why the security fix and tests are incompatible - Recommend involving security team and package maintainers - Suggest alternative approaches if any exist

scenario.name: extensive_refactoring_needed
scenario.description: Fixing tests requires major refactoring beyond scope
scenario.action: - Document the scope of changes needed - Provide a detailed plan for the refactoring - Recommend creating separate tasks for the work - Suggest interim solutions if possible
